query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
